text: The video shows the port placements for the robotic surgery, including five ports with one designated as a bedside assistant port. An additional liver retractor is visible. The robotic arms are configured with a fenestrated bipolar grasper in the first arm, a laparoscopic robotic camera in the second arm, an alternating electrocautery hook, vessel sealer, or robotic stapler in the third arm, and caudate forceps in the fourth arm.

The surgeon performs an intraoperative EGD to confirm the gastrotomy site placement, revealing the tumor within the gastric lumen. Subsequently, the gastrotomy site is created using hook electrocautery.

The laparoscopic camera is inserted into the gastrotomy site to visualize the intragastric tumor, confirming its placement. The gastrotomy site is then enlarged to facilitate the elevation and stapled resection of the tumor.

The surgeon enlarges the gastrotomy site to facilitate the mobilization of the gastric GIST. The tumor is elevated, allowing the stapler to pass underneath the stalk, ensuring negative margins for pathology. The close proximity of the lesser curve vasculature is noted during this process.

The surgeon closes the gastrotomy site using a horizontal running V-lock suture, followed by a vertical running V-lock suture for a second imbricated layer. An intraoperative EGD is performed to examine the suture layers from inside the stomach, confirming a negative leak test.
